name: Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir North Route
description: This 20.0-mile (32.2 km) mountain biking route around Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir offers scenic views and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia, located in Álava, Basque Country, Spain, offers diverse and well-maintained trails. The region is characterized by the expansive reservoir, surrounded by picturesque landscapes, riverside forests, and green spaces. The terrain includes mostly flat circular paths around the reservoir, as well as unpaved segments with varying elevations for more rugged experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta al embalse de Ullíbarri-Gamboa, a 20.0 miles (32.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This circular route encircles the reservoir, offering scenic views and a comfortable ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri – circular desde Muskurio, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This route passes through riverside forests and natural areas near the reservoir's edges. Local mountain bikers also love the Etxe-Zuri Bar – Albertia Memorial Monument loop from Subero, a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia is defined by the expansive reservoir, lush riverside forests, and diverse trail surfaces. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 8 easy, 44 moderate, and 23 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia is diverse. You'll find mostly flat, comfortable circular paths, especially around the reservoir itself. However, there are also unpaved segments with varying elevations for those seeking a more rugged mountain biking experience. Some routes pass through riverside forests and natural areas, offering a mix of surfaces.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area offers several family-friendly options. The main circular path around the reservoir, approximately 44 kilometers, is mostly flat and comfortable, making it popular for families. Shorter circuits, such as a 13 km or 32 km route, are also available. An easy route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38228561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barracones Reservoir – Landa Lakeside Path loop from Landa&lt;/a&gt;, at 14.2 km, is a good choice for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around the reservoir?
A: Yes, circular routes are a highlight of mountain biking here. A popular option is the approximately 44-kilometer path that encircles the entire reservoir. For a slightly shorter, moderate circular ride, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077725380/colas-de-embalse-ullibarri-circular-desde-muskurio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri – circular desde Muskurio&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.8 km long and takes about 54 minutes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer views of the expansive reservoir and its islands, including Zuhatza. You can also pass through the Provincial Parks of Garaio and Landa, which feature Blue Flag certified beaches. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/310765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedestrian and cycle bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1593201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azua Footbridge&lt;/a&gt;, which cross sections of the reservoir. The area is also a Natura 2000 site, known for its rich biodiversity and riverside forests.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in this region?
A: The Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia area is enjoyable for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer is also popular, especially with the nearby beaches for cooling off, but can be warmer. Winter rides are possible, but check local conditions for rain or colder weather.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain bike trails using public transport from Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Yes, the reservoir is easily accessible from Vitoria-Gasteiz, which is just over 14 kilometers away. Public transport options are available, and dedicated cycle lanes connect Vitoria-Gasteiz directly to the area, making it convenient to bring your bike or rent one upon arrival.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 23 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40372535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxe-Zuri Bar – Albertia Memorial Monument loop from Subero&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.9 km trail with significant elevation gain, typically completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails here?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 145 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse trail surfaces, the beautiful reservoir views, and the lush riverside forests that make for a tranquil natural setting.

Q: Are there places to rent bicycles near Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia?
A: Yes, bicycle rental is available in Garaio, which is one of the key recreational areas located around the reservoir. This makes it convenient for visitors who do not bring their own bikes.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails?
A: While the region is generally dog-friendly, specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural areas like the Natura 2000 site and Ramsar wetland, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking at Embalse de Ullibarri / Uribarriko urtegia?
A: The Provincial Parks of Garaio and Landa, located around the reservoir, are key recreational areas and typically offer parking facilities. These parks serve as excellent starting points for many of the trails, providing convenient access and amenities.
tips: Landa has some beautiful fields for sunbathing or for flying kites. Sport that is increasingly seen in these areas. There are different bars where you can eat very well, such as Etxezuri, which is located in Landa, or Erpidea, which is in Ullibarri Ganboa. It is also very close to Vitoria by bike.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Vuelta al embalse de Ullíbarri-Gamboa mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as moderate for mountain biking, suggesting good fitness is required but it's suitable for all skill levels. It's largely considered low to moderate difficulty, making it accessible for a wide range of cyclists, including families. The route has a relatively low elevation gain, with most of it being flat with good ground.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse range of surfaces. You'll primarily ride on well-trodden dirt paths, but also encounter singletrack (17.2 km), paved sections (18.7 km), cycleways (5.42 km), and some road sections (2.12 km). There are also wooden walkways and occasional stretches with loose stones, especially on the single notable ascent and descent.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers continuous scenic views of the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir. Key highlights include the Landa Lakeside Path, a View of Barracones Reservoir, and a View of the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir and village. You'll also pass through diverse ecosystems, including open fields and dense wooded areas, and skirt the Mendixur Ornithological Park and the inland beaches of Álava.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta al embalse de Ullíbarri-Gamboa suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is highly recommended for families. Its generally low difficulty, well-marked, and circular nature make it very accessible. Shorter, more family-oriented options exist, such as the 13 km "Vuelta al humedal de las colas del embalse." However, be aware that it can get busy on good weather weekends, making some narrower sections challenging with children.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible, particularly from Vitoria-Gasteiz. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, it's common for popular reservoir routes to have designated parking areas near access points, especially around the Landa and Garaio Provincial Parks. You can also extend your ride from Vitoria-Gasteiz via a greenway.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vuelta al embalse de Ullíbarri-Gamboa is generally dog-friendly. However, due to its popularity, especially on weekends, you might encounter many pedestrians, children, and other dogs. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to ensure safety and respect for other trail users and wildlife, particularly near the Mendixur Ornithological Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for combining biking with swimming in the reservoir. Weekends, particularly in good weather, can be very busy, so consider visiting on weekdays or earlier in the morning to avoid crowds.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to use the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and mountain bike the Vuelta al embalse de Ullíbarri-Gamboa trail. It's a publicly accessible route around the reservoir.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers 97% of the "Ruta Norte del embalse Ullibarri-Gamboa" and 97% of the "Vuelta completa al embalse Ullibarri-Gamboa." It also briefly touches sections of the "Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea" and the "Lautadako Bira," among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes or accommodation directly on the trail aren't detailed, the route is easily accessible from Vitoria-Gasteiz, which offers a full range of amenities. The Landa and Garaio Provincial Parks, which the trail skirts, are popular recreational areas and likely have facilities or are close to villages with services.

faqs.question: Is there a specific section of the trail that is more challenging?
faqs.answer: The majority of the route is flat with good ground. However, there is one significant climb where riders might need to exert considerable effort to avoid dismounting. This is the most challenging part of an otherwise generally easy to moderate ride.

waypoints.name: View of the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir and village
